selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and to take steps to protect yourself. One way to do this is by using fact-checking websites and third-party verification tools to verify the authenticity of content before sharing it. Additionally, setting your social media platforms to only allow posts from friends and family can help limit exposure to fake content. It's also important to be mindful of the language used in posts and to avoid engaging with or sharing content that seems suspicious or too good to be true. By taking these steps, you can better manage and deal with fake content on social media.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and to take steps to protect yourself. One way to do this is by using fact-checking websites and third-party verification tools to verify the authenticity of content before sharing it. Additionally, setting your social media platforms to only allow posts from friends and family can help limit exposure to fake content. It's also important to be mindful of the language used in posts and to avoid engaging with or sharing content that seems suspicious or too good to be true. By taking these steps, you can better manage and deal with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and to take steps to protect yourself. One way to do this is by using fact-checking websites and third-party verification tools to verify the authenticity of content before sharing it. Additionally, setting your social media platforms to only allow posts from friends and family can help limit exposure to fake content. It's also important to be mindful of the language used in posts and to avoid engaging with or sharing content that seems suspicious or too good to be true. By taking these steps, you can better manage and deal with fake content on social media.
